name: KrillApp.DataPoint
title: Data Points
description: Represents a single measurable value that stores time-series data. Receives snapshot updates containing a value and timestamp, validates them against child filter nodes, stores validated snapshots in the data store, and triggers downstream execution. Supports configurable data types (text, JSON, digital, double), precision, units, and manual entry. Child nodes can include filters, graphs, triggers, and executors.
shortDescription: Stores time-series snapshot values and triggers downstream nodes on data updates.
llmPurpose: Stores time-series snapshot data and propagates updates to child filters, triggers, executors, and graphs.
llmBehavior: stores validated snapshots in persistent data store
executes child nodes on data update
llmInputs: configuration: data type, precision, unit, maxAge
llmTypicalUseCases: feed data to triggers and calculations
llmPromptHints: create a data point for this sensor
llmCreationHints: Create a DataPoint when the user needs to store and track a measurable value over time. Configure data type, precision, and unit. Add child filters, triggers, or graphs as needed.

llmConnectionHints.role: central data node that feeds triggers, filters, graphs, and executors

llmExamples: Create a data point to store temperature readings.
Add a data point for this serial sensor with 2 decimal precision.
Track humidity values over time with a unit of '%'.
Set up a data point that I can manually enter values into.
